title: Network Flow, Cuts, and Bipartite Matching · Complexity Checkpoint
summary: Assign workers to constrained jobs and identify capacity bottlenecks. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a flow-based assignment model and cut explanation. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate capacities, conservation, source, sink, and residual choices from a stakeholder problem.
Trace forward and reverse residual capacity after augmentation.
Find augmenting paths and update flow without violating capacity or conservation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Flow Model correctness-and-cost proof for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Flow Model assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Assign workers {A,B,C} to jobs {1,2,3} with one forbidden pair and capacity 3. Trace residual changes, reconstruct matched pairs, and use a cut as optimality evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Flow Model has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
